trending_posts.author: @dioscuri
trending_posts.name: Henry Shevlin
trending_posts.post: Big personal news: I’ve been recruited by Google DeepMind for a new Philosopher position (actual title), focusing on machine consciousness, human-AI relationships, and AGI readiness, starting in May. I’ll continue my research & teaching at Cambridge part-time. Absolutely stoked!

trending_posts.author: @OpenAI
trending_posts.name: OpenAI
trending_posts.post:
We recently identified a security issue involving the third-party developer library Axios that was part of a broader industry incident. We found no evidence that OpenAI user data was accessed, that our systems were compromised, or that our software was altered.

Out of an abundance of caution we are taking steps to protect the process that certifies our macOS applications are legitimate OpenAI apps.

We are updating our security certifications, which will require all macOS users to update their OpenAI apps to the latest versions. This helps prevent any risk—however unlikely—of someone attempting to distribute a fake app that appears to be from OpenAI. You can update safely through an in-app update or at the official links below. 🧵

Anthropic Fellows Program - 4-month, fully funded research fellowship at Anthropic for promising talent in AI safety, security & economics/societal impact

Fellows receive a weekly stipend of $3,850 USD, ~$15K/month in compute, direct mentorship from Anthropic researchers & access to workspaces in Berkeley or London 

Must have work authorization in the US, UK or Canada. Prior AI safety experience not required. 

Deadline: April 26 (for July 2026 cohort)

briefing.community_pulse:
• A major controversy erupted over AI in journalism when @DHSgov accused @ABC7 of using AI to edit official statements, removing details about an illegal alien's criminal background to fit a narrative bias. In their post, @DHSgov stated: "There are no journalistic 'ethics and standards' that empower the media to leave out key facts. Furthermore, they decided to shamelessly lie in their article and claim that DHS did not respond to their inquiry."[[1]](https://x.com/i/status/2042700741396025468) This sparked debates on ideological censorship and media ethics.

• The Linux community debated the use of AI-generated code, with @Pirat_Nation reporting that maintainers allow tools like GitHub Copilot but reject low-quality "AI slop," emphasizing: "Humans take the fall for mistakes." The post highlights responsibility backed by Linus Torvalds, fueling discussions on AI's role in software development.[[2]](https://x.com/i/status/2043404282884395012)

• AI safety advocate @AISafetyMemes called out a conflict of interest in government contracts, alleging: "This dude gave xAI huge government contracts while DIRECTLY OWNING xAI shares. He profited up to $24 million. Oh, AND he's the same guy trying to murder Anthropic."[[3]](https://x.com/i/status/2042651193122398663) This ignited controversy over ethics, profiteering, and competition in the AI industry.

• A debate arose around AI in creative production when @TheePopCore clarified that the "Bad Angel" music video by Anyma & LISA used CGI and VFX, not AI, with director Gianluigi Carella stating: "Not aware this was a debate but yes an uncalculable amount of hours, sweat and blood was poured into this."[[4]](https://x.com/i/status/2042462082319466789) This addressed misconceptions and sparked talks on distinguishing AI from traditional effects.

• Discussions on AI and intellectual property heated up with @openart_ai's announcement of an IP Safety Check tool, explaining: "First AI platform to build this natively. Because 'can we actually use this?' deserves a real answer."[[5]](https://x.com/i/status/2042731283982283166) The feature flags issues in generated images, contributing to ongoing debates about copyright and ethical AI use.
